overViewOfOnline.subHeading: Blend HR Expertise with Powerful Business Analytics Capabilities
overViewOfOnline.description: The MBA in Human Resource and Business Analytics at Teksversity equips learners with the dual expertise needed for modern HR and data-driven organizational decision-making. Students learn HR analytics, workforce forecasting, business insights, performance modelling, and employee lifecycle metrics through real datasets and guided analytical tools. The curriculum integrates HR management principles with business analytics applications, strengthening your ability to solve workplace challenges using data. This flexible program supports working professionals in India seeking HR analytics, talent insights, workforce planning, and strategic HR roles across industries
keyPoints: Understand HR metrics, forecasting, and analytics
Use BI dashboards, HR tools, and predictive models
Strengthen analytical and HR decision-making skills
Apply ethical and effective HR analytics practices
Build readiness for HR analytics and HRBP roles

heading: Why Choose MBA in Human Resource and Business Analytics
in Teksversity?
whyOnline.initialDescription: Teksversity delivers a specialized MBA combining HR principles with business analytics, enabling learners to lead organizational decision-making through data. The curriculum focuses on talent analytics, workforce metrics, dashboard design, and predictive insights using real datasets and analytical tools.

description.firstDescription: Students are guided by experienced HR and analytics experts who help them build capabilities in modelling HR trends, identifying workforce gaps, predicting attrition, and optimizing performance outcomes. With hands-on case tasks and simulations, learners build confidence in applying analytics to HR operations.

description.secondDescription: Designed for working professionals and HR-focused graduates, this online MBA provides flexibility without compromising practical experience. With resume building, mock interviews, and career assistance, Teksversity ensures learners are ready for HR analytics, HRBP analytics, workforce planning, and business strategy roles.

testimonialCards.review: This Dual Certification course in Digital Marketing and Business Analytics is fantastic. The trainers are knowledgeable and the practical projects helped me apply concepts effectively. A must for anyone looking to excel in marketing and analytics!

testimonialCards.name: sadhana chittennoju
testimonialCards.review: I enrolled in the online Data Science course at Teksversity in March 2025. The Python, ML modules, real-world projects, and weekly assignments were very useful. Good placement support. It helped me build a strong portfolio. Great for career switchers

testimonialCards.course: Data Science with AI and ML

testimonialCards.name: Gayathri Prayaga

faq.question: What is an MBA in Human Resource & Business Analytics?
faq.answer: An MBA in Human Resource & Business Analytics is a dual-specialization program that combines people management with data-driven decision-making. It trains students to optimize workforce performance, analyze HR metrics, and make strategic HR decisions using analytics tools.

faq.question: What skills will I gain from this program?
faq.answer: You will gain skills in HR analytics, workforce planning, recruitment strategy, employee engagement, performance management, predictive HR modeling, data visualization, Excel, SQL, Python, Power BI, and Tableau making you ready for modern HR and analytics roles.

faq.question: What subjects are included in the MBA syllabus?
faq.answer: The curriculum typically covers Human Resource Management, Organizational Behavior, Talent Acquisition, Compensation & Benefits, HR Analytics, Workforce Planning, Predictive Analytics, Business Intelligence Tools, Performance Management, and Employee Engagement Strategies.

faq.question: What career opportunities are available after this MBA?
faq.answer: Graduates can pursue roles such as HR Analyst, HR Manager, Talent Acquisition Specialist, Learning & Development Manager, People Analytics Specialist, Recruitment Consultant, HR Business Partner, and HR Data Analyst in corporate firms, consulting, IT, and analytics-driven organizations.

faq.question: What salary can I expect after completing this MBA?
faq.answer: Fresh graduates can expect ₹5 LPA to ₹10 LPA on average. Professionals skilled in HR analytics or predictive modeling may secure higher packages in leading MNCs, IT firms, and global consulting companies.

faq.question: Is this course suitable for students without a technical background?
faq.answer: Yes. The program introduces foundational analytics and gradually builds advanced HR analytics concepts, making it accessible to students from management, commerce, or other non-technical backgrounds.

faq.question: What tools and technologies will I learn?
faq.answer: Students gain hands-on experience with Excel, SQL, Python, R basics, Power BI, Tableau, HR analytics platforms, predictive modeling tools, and business intelligence software commonly used in modern HR departments.

faq.question: Does this MBA provide placement assistance?
faq.answer: Yes. Institutes offer complete placement support, including resume building, mock interviews, aptitude training, mentorship, industry tie-ups, HR workshops, and campus recruitment opportunities.

faq.question: How does the MBA in HR and Business Analytics prepare students for industry roles?
faq.answer: This HR-focused specialization combines people management and analytics, building skills in talent acquisition, workforce analytics, employee engagement, and data-driven HR strategies.

faq.question: Is an MBA in Human Resource & Business Analytics a good career choice?
faq.answer: Absolutely. With businesses increasingly relying on data-driven HR decisions, this dual specialization offers strong career growth, high demand, competitive salaries, and excellent long-term prospects, making it one of the most future-ready MBA options.
